channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: The worst idea in the history of tobacco control
item.description: Putting nicotine into a global drug prohibition treaty would be one of the most damaging ideas in tobacco control. It would treat smoke-free alternatives like illicit drugs while leaving the central cause of harm — combustible tobacco — dangerously misunderstood. Dependence matters, but policy must distinguish between addiction and the disease risks caused by smoking. A global nicotine ban would not end demand; it would push safer products underground and protect cigarettes by default. (Published on July 07, 2026bysubstack.com)

item.title: Ignorance Leads To Policy Fails
item.description: Poor understanding of nicotine and relative risk leads to poor policy. When decision-makers treat vaping, pouches and cigarettes as if they carry the same harms, regulation can end up discouraging smokers from switching while leaving combustible tobacco protected by default. Public health decisions should be based on evidence, not confusion or moral panic. Ignorance does not reduce harm — it creates policy failure. (Published on July 07, 2026byplanetofthevapes.co.uk)

item.title: Tobacco Harm Reduction IS Harm Reduction-Part 4
item.description: Tobacco harm reduction is harm reduction. The principle is the same: reduce risk, respect autonomy and meet people where they are, rather than demanding abstinence as the only acceptable outcome. For people who smoke, access to lower-risk nicotine products can offer a practical route away from combustible tobacco. Public health should apply harm reduction consistently — including when the substance is nicotine. (Published on July 08, 2026bysubstack.com)
